briefing.title: Market-Leading Sell-Through
briefing.detail: Purse Maison leads all stores with 40 units sold MTD (₱12.21M revenue), nearly 4× the nearest volume competitor Miss Manila Luxe (69 units but at ₱77K avg ticket vs our ₱179K), confirming dominance in the mid-to-high tier.
briefing.action: Maintain current momentum but monitor Miss Manila Luxe's 69-unit pace — identify which of their 'Other' brand models (73.4% mix) are moving to spot any entry-level demand we can capture with faster-turning accessories.

briefing.title: Hermès Birkin & Kelly Drive Revenue
briefing.detail: Hermès (40.5% of inventory) and Birkin/Kelly top our MTD sold list; Shop with K also converted Birkin and Constance at a ₱333K avg ticket vs our ₱179K, suggesting a premium-willing buyer segment we are underserving.
briefing.action: Audit our Birkin and Kelly listings priced above ₱1M to confirm they are positioned at or above market comps — do not undercut Shop with K on exotic/rare pieces; compete on curation and authentication credibility instead.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo Overstock Risk
briefing.detail: We carry 23 Jumbo units (our largest single-model exposure) with a lowest ask of ₱188K matching the market floor; the market has 36 live units total, signaling high supply pressure across all stores.
briefing.action: Flag any Jumbo units sitting >21 days (2× our 11.1-day avg) for a ₱10K–₱15K markdown to clear before month-end and free capital for faster-turning Birkin/Kelly restocks.

briefing.title: Birkin 25 & Kelly 25 Pricing Premium vs Market
briefing.detail: Our Birkin 25 lowest ask is ₱1.288M vs market low of ₱950K (+35.6%); our Kelly 25 lowest ask is ₱1.15M vs market low of ₱850K (+35.3%) — we are holding significant premiums on both.
briefing.action: Validate the premium is justified by hardware/leather/condition differentiation; if not, move lowest-priced units to ₱1.05–₱1.10M range to stay competitive while protecting margin on exotic pieces.

briefing.title: Luxury Wish Stall — Competitive Opportunity
briefing.detail: Luxury Wish recorded 0 sales MTD despite 159 units (₱47.26M inventory) and a historically fast 4.9-day avg sell time — their Chanel-heavy mix (44.8%) is stagnant, possibly due to pricing or listing issues.
briefing.action: Cross-check if their Chanel Double Flap listings have been re-priced upward; if buyers are being pushed out, be ready to absorb demand with competitive Chanel pricing on our 7 Double Flap Small and 12 Medium units.

briefing.title: Inventory Depth vs Capital Efficiency
briefing.detail: Purse Maison holds ₱286.4M in inventory across 1,595 units — 3.4× The Bag Hub's inventory value on similar unit count, and our sell-through rate is only 2.5% of inventory sold MTD (40/1,595).
briefing.action: Run an inventory age report on the 28.4% 'Other' brand segment; any non-hero models beyond 30 days should receive a 10–15% price reduction to improve capital velocity ahead of Q3.

alerts: ⚠️ Birkin 30 lowest ask at ₱770K is 33.9% above market low of ₱575K — with 17 market units live, we risk being skipped entirely on price-sensitive searches; review immediately.

🚨 Mini Kelly (Kelly 20): our lowest ask ₱1.54M exceeds market high of ₱1.68M by only ₱140K but is 58.8% above market low of ₱970K — only 4 market units total, treat as scarce; hold price but ensure listing visibility is maximized.

📈 Shop with K sold ₱4.8M MTD on only 503 units at ₱333K avg ticket — their Constance and Della Cavalleria conversions suggest an ultra-premium buyer segment actively transacting this month; consider spotlighting our 10 Constance 18 units.

⚡ The Bag Hub is moving entry-level units fast (7.7-day avg, Bottega Jodie, LV Multi Pochette, SYL WOC) — this demand for sub-₱200K accessible luxury is not being captured by Purse Maison; evaluate stocking 5–10 such models.

🔍 Canone Bags Prime shows 0.0 avg days to sell with 8 units moved — data anomaly or same-day flipping activity; monitor for aggressive below-market pricing that could pressure our LV and entry Chanel segment.

pricing.suggestion: Hold premium positioning but bring lowest ask down from ₱1.288M to ₱1.10–₱1.15M on entry-condition units; reserve ₱1.3M+ for exotic leathers, given market low of ₱950K and 18 competing live listings.
Reduce lowest ask from ₱770K closer to ₱650–₱680K to narrow the 33.9% gap above the ₱575K market floor; with 17 market units live, price competitiveness is critical to avoid prolonged days-on-market.

Our lowest ask of ₱735K sits 53% above the ₱480K market floor in a 28-unit deep market — tier pricing by condition: entry units to ₱520–₱560K, mid to ₱700–₱900K, exotics above ₱1M, to cover the full buyer spectrum.

With 23 units and market floor at ₱188K (which we match), differentiate upward on condition and hardware — price C-grade units at ₱188–₱210K for velocity, A-grade at ₱280–₱320K; avoid a race to the bottom in a 36-unit market.

At ₱404K we are already at the market floor with 10 units live — given Shop with K's active Constance conversions this month, test raising 2–3 pristine units to ₱480–₱520K while keeping baseline units at ₱404–₱420K.

Our lowest ask of ₱534.5K is 35.3% above the ₱395K market low in a 16-unit market — either validate condition premium or bring entry units to ₱420–₱450K to remain in buyer shortlists without ceding full margin.
